column2: et intendit mihī. ^ With expectation I have waited for the Lord
column3: and he was attentive to me.

column2: et ēduxit mē dē lacū miseriae et dē lutō faecis. Et statūvit super petram pedēs meōs
column3: et dīrexit gressūs meōs. ^ And he heard my prayers
column4: and brought me out of the pit of misery and the mire of dregs. And he set my feet upon a rock

column1: 4 ^ Et immīsit in ōs meum canticum novum
column4: et spērābunt in Dominō. ^ And he put a new canticle into my mouth
column5: a song to our God. Many shall see
column7: and they shall hope in the Lord.

column1: 5 ^ Beātus vir cujus est nōmen Dominī spēs ejus
column2: et non respexit in vānitātēs et insāniās falsās. ^ Blessed is the man whose trust is in the name of the Lord
column3: and who hath not had regard to vanities and lying follies.

column4: et cōgitātiōnibus tuīs non est quī similis sit tibī. Adnuntiāvī et locūtus sum~; multiplicātī sunt super numerum. ^ Many things thou hast wrought
column7: and in thy thoughts there is no one like to thee. I have declared and I have spoken~; they are multiplied above number.

column1: 7 ^ Sacrificium et oblātiōnem nōluistī~; aurēs autem perfēcistī mihī. Holocaustum et prō peccātō non postulāvistī. ^ Sacrifice and oblation thou didst not desire~; but thou hast perfected ears for me. Burnt offering and sin offering thou didst not require.

column2: «~Ecce veniō~; in capite librī scriptum est dē mē
column4: "Behold I come~; in the head of the book it is written of me

column1: 9 ^ ut facerem voluntātem tuam~; Deus meus
column3: et lēgem tuam in mediō cordis meī.~»^ that I should do thy will~; O my God
column5: and thy law in the midst of my heart."

column1: 10 ^ Adnuntiāvī jūstitiam tuam in ecclēsiā magnā~; ecce labia mea non prōhibēbō~; Domine
column2: tū scīvistī. ^ I have declared thy justice in a great church~; behold I will not restrain my lips~; O Lord

column1: 11 ^ Jūstitiam tuam non abscondī in corde meō~; vēritātem tuam et salūtāre tuum dixī. Non absconduī misericordiam tuam et vēritātem tuam ā conciliō multō. ^ I have not hid thy justice within my heart~; I have declared thy truth and thy salvation. I have not concealed thy mercy and thy truth from a great council.

column3: nē longē faciās miserātiōnēs tuās ā mē~; misericordia tua et vēritās tua semper suscēpērunt mē. ^ Withhold not thou
column5: thy tender mercies from me~; thy mercy and thy truth have always upheld me.

column1: 13 ^ Quoniam circumdedērunt mē mala quōrum non est numerus~; comprehendērunt mē inīquitātēs meae
column2: et non potuī ut vidērem~; multiplicātae sunt super capillōs capitis meī
column3: et cor meum dērelīquit mē. ^ For evils without number have surrounded me~; my iniquities have overtaken me
column4: and I was not able to see~; they are multiplied above the hairs of my head
column5: and my heart hath forsaken me.

column4: ad adjuvandum mē respice. ^ Be pleased

column3: ut auferant eam~; convertantur retrorsum et revereantur
column4: quī volunt mihī mala. ^ Let them be confounded and ashamed together
column5: who seek after my soul to take it away~; let them be turned backward and be ashamed

column4: eugē.~»^ Let them immediately bear their confusion

column1: 17 ^ Exsultent et laetentur super tē omnēs quaerentēs tē
column4: quī dīligunt salūtāre tuum. ^ Let all that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee
column7:
who love thy salvation.
18 ^ Egō autem mendīcus sum et pauper~; Dominus sollicitus est meī. Adjūtor meus et prōtector meus tū es~; Deus meus, nē tardāverīs. ^ But I am a beggar and poor~; the Lord is careful for me. Thou art my helper and my protector~; O my God, be not tarried.
